title: agave toumeyana subsp. bella
intro: Agave toumeyana subsp. bella, also known as the Bella Tooth-Ache or Tooth-Ache Agave, is a succulent plant species native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat toothaches and other mouth-related issues due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Agave toumeyana subsp. bella include its potential to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria, making it a natural remedy for mouth infections, gum disease, and toothaches. The plant is often prepared as a poultice or salve, which can be applied topically to the affected area, and can also be made into a tincture or infused oil for internal use.
